config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, keputusan yang terlalu mengutamakan “demi masyarakat” kadang dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan terhadap orang kecil atau kelompok minoritas. Yang ideal adalah mencari keseimbangan: masyarakat kuat, tetapi hak dasar tiap orang tetap dilindungi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people emphasize social stability, public order, and collective responsibility, but I also know how easily “for the greater good” can be used to silence ordinary people or excuse unfair treatment. A society is not truly healthy if individuals have no real protection against the majority or against authority. The better principle is that social interests matter, but only within clear limits that protect basic individual rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara yang lemah, padahal justru hak individu diperlukan agar mayoritas dan negara tidak bertindak semena-mena. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi hanya sah bila dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas, seperti kebebasan berpendapat, perlakuan adil, dan perlindungan dari diskriminasi. Jadi, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat versus individu, melainkan mencari keseimbangan yang tidak mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have automatic priority over the individual, even though I value social harmony and public order. From my perspective in China, people often see the benefits of collective action, but I also know that once “society first” becomes a blanket rule, it can easily justify ignoring ordinary people’s dignity and interests. A strong society should protect individuals, because rights are what keep collective power from becoming arbitrary. So I would say social interests matter, but they should be limited by firm individual rights, not placed above them by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan terhadap orang kecil, bukan untuk benar-benar melindungi kepentingan umum. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, terutama saat menyangkut keselamatan, ketertiban, atau bencana, tetapi itu tidak boleh menjadi alasan otomatis untuk menginjak hak dasar individu. Justru hak individu adalah pagar supaya mayoritas, aparat, atau kelompok kuat tidak semena-mena. Jadi bagi saya, yang benar bukan mendahulukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan hak dasar sebagai batas yang tegas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the claim that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value harmony, order, and collective goals, but I have also seen how easily “for society’s sake” can become a shortcut for ignoring ordinary people’s dignity and basic protections. A society may need temporary limits in emergencies, but that is very different from making individual rights secondary by default. In my view, a healthy society is one where collective interests are pursued within firm boundaries that protect each person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
